item.title: New PATCH Released + CONSOLE Announced 📢
item.description:
DA MEK BOYZ BE FIXIN'⚙️

We've just dropped a new patch on Steam. Here's the full change list:

  • Join/invite through Steam

    Players can now use the Steam UI to invite their friend to the match their in, or join a match their friend is already in

  • An option to have unrestricted team switching when creating a match

    When this is enabled, players can switch teams regardless of scores, as long as there is a spot on the other team. This allows friends to more easily play on the same team

  • Improvements to bot behaviour, including having Squigbuggies focus on healing much more. Bots also use dash and some abilities more.

  • Significant gamepad input improvements

    Including driving, aim assist, and adding a new trigger driving setup as an alternative to joystick driving. We've also made gamepad fully usable in Creation Mode

  • Fixed issue with clients sometimes not joining the new match when the host is migrated

  • Fixed issue with UI disappearing in certain cases

  • Friendly Squigbuggies now appear as a blue + on the HUD instead of a dot

  • Added race waypoint counter to Kustom Rally and Creation Mode

  • Various UI and flow improvements, as well as bug fixes

  • Optimizations to Ork characters

  • Various other small fixes/improvements

  • Deffkopta can no longer stun itself with its own bomb, and fixed an issue with flying speed. Also increased overall health by 5%

  • Trukk now has +15 health and does 5% more damage per second

Onto da pretty big newz...

DEZE ORKS IZ COMIN' TO CONSOLEZ 🎮
In case you missed it, Speed Freeks is on its way to Playstation 5 and XBOX Series X/S this spring!

As mentioned in our previous announcement, development of these versions has been going on for a long time, but they're finally coming.

This means CROSSPLAY between all three platforms, and more gits fer you'z to krump or team up wiv!

Along with that console release we'll be patching in a new WAAAGH! Path full of free items on Steam as well 🤘

Maybe you can guess the type of Orks that are comin' with it as Pit Bosses? The first three to guess correctly get a Speed Freeks Steam key to give to a friend 💥

Here's the console announcement from the official Warhammer channel:

So far we've seen great reception to the announcement, so you can expect plenty of gits to be JOININ' DA WAAAGH! once the console versions release 🔥

There will be an FAQ in the future about crossplay and anything else pertaining to the console release (that is relevant to you guys)

Hope you like the latest changes

Happy krumpin' ladz

item.title: Dev Blog - Ogryn Pack Master
item.description:
Warband,

 
Today we have a treat for you all, not one, but two new enemies are on their way to make a debut in Darktide! We have braved the mad laboratory lair of Jonathan, our Game Designer in charge of these new creations.

Take it away!

“Hello! My name is Jonathan and I have been told I am not good at making enemies in real life, so I took it as my mission to make them for the game instead. Jokes aside, I work with stuff relating to enemy behavior, pacing management and general enemy combat design.” 

  • Jonathan

We have two new enemies to talk about today, both of them being themed to each other. You might have already seen them in the {LINK REMOVED}new teaser video {LINK REMOVED}here. Let’s shine the spotlight on them, one at a time, starting with the…

Ogryn Pack Master

Have you ever wondered who takes care of all those Pox Hounds running around in Tertium? Well this might be your answer, introducing the Pack Master, an Ogryn monstrosity (yeah you read that right) that packs a serious punch!

The Pack Master ticks two boxes for us, one is the fact that we wanted to add another monstrosity to the enemy roster for some while now, and the other is the fact we have wanted to make a monstrosity that is a bit less daemonic or mutated. The game hasn't had a new monstrosity in ages, so it was about time. 

Making him an Ogryn also lets us have a monstrosity with interesting voice lines that are not just guttural roars or chaotic screams, but instead a guy who likes to talk while he battles the players. He has some really cool reactive voice lines depending on what happens around him. He will for example react when you take one of his hounds down or when one of them manages to pounce you or someone on your team.

But you are not here to read just about his “lovely” personality, you wanna know what he brings to the fight right!

“He is very fast for an Ogryn. Very fast and very heavy, and he will keep up with you!” 

  • Jonathan

The Pack Master is a fast and heavy bruiser type of an enemy. He won’t have any problem charging into the fray, his big shocking prod ready to bash some agents of the Imperium. He is a melee fighter that will do his utmost to get into your face. This weapon also applies a debuff to players, so getting hit will not just hurt, but also leave you with a speed reduction and slower dodge replenishment.

Additionally, what makes him extra scary is that he will be joined by  Pox Hounds to help him in combat. Depending on the situation, he can bring along either normal Pox Hounds, the new Armoured Pox Hound (more on that in a bit) or a mix of the two. He is called the Pack Master for a reason! It will also be important to take him down as quickly as possible, since even if you get rid of all of his hounds, he can still spawn in new ones as long as he is alive.

Another interesting thing is that you will find him roaming, so you will be able to hear him as he walks around. You can use this to your advantage and prepare to take him on. Having all of those hounds around him makes him a pretty good tracker, so hiding from him might prove very hard. 

“Oh and he sees through stealth! This is a big one. You can't hide from him, he will see you and just keep up his attack.” 

  • Jonathan


Finally, before we move the spotlight to his canine friend, we should give a fair warning to all Arbites players out there. This guy is used to having unruly dogs around him, so you should probably think twice before you send your Cyber Mastiff his way. Don’t say we didn’t warn you. 

“Yeah we do have a new cool animation for when a Cyber-Mastiff attacks the Pack Master and he just…” 

  • Jonathan

With those words of warning, let’s proceed to the…

Armoured Pox Hound

So this is a new Pox Hound with, you guessed it, armour. This enemy is what we could call a sidegrade to the normal Pox Hound. All that armour is giving it more health than the already existing hound, so it’s harder to take down, but at the same time all that metal makes it slower than its less iron clad cousin.

If you manage to block this enemy in midair you can stagger it, and if you do, you might want to circle around  and attack the unarmoured weak spot on their backs for some extra damage. We hope that this new variant will give even the most seasoned Pox Hounds hunters a new challenge! 

“Best thing is to stagger them and then circle around and hit them in the… yeah, haha” 

  • Jonathan

The armoured variety will be part of the Pack Master’s pack, so you will only encounter these hounds when he is around.

Hope this little teaser has made you ready to fight the Pack Master when he and his new armoured friends enter the fight in Darktide. They will both appear in the next update, and while we’re excited to share more, we’ll dive into the details when the time is right!

So for now we will have to leave this as just a teaser! Keep your eyes on our community space and social media channels for information when we are ready to talk more about this! 

In the meantime, as always, thank you for reading this dev blog.

See you on the Mourningstar! 

- The Darktide Team

item.title: CROSS-PLAY with Consoles is Coming 🔃
item.description:
OI GITS!

We can't believe it either, but the PlayStation & XBOX versions of Speed Freeks are finally getting wrapped up for release this spring 🤘

The console version development actually started in 2023 and despite some big bumps along the way, they're almost here.

Ok, so what?

What does this mean for PC players? Well, more players in general, and thus more matches to be found.

The biggest complaint we see about the game is the lack of players to play with, so this will be a huge win in that department.

The console release will also come with a new WAAAGH! Path full of items, which is obviously available to Steam players as well (for free, of course) 👍

Next Patch 🛠️

We're soon releasing a regular update to the Steam version before the console release, so you can expect that in the next couple weeks. The main highlights of this patch are:

  • Join/invite through Steam

    Players can now use the Steam UI to invite their friend to the match their in, or join a match their friend is already in

  • An option to have unrestricted switching teams when creating a match

    When this is enabled, players can switch teams regardless of scores, as long as there is a spot on the other team. This allows friends to more easily play on the same team

  • Improvements to bot behaviour, including having Squigbuggies focus on healing more (plus friendly squigbuggies now have a + sign on HUD instead of a dot)

  • Significant gamepad improvements, including driving, aim assist, and adding a new trigger driving setup as an alternative to joystick driving

  • Workshop and Kustom Rally UI and flow improvements

  • Deffkopta can no longer stun itself

  • And probably other stuff I'm forgetting

We'll have full patch notes once it drops.

Here's the PlayStation announcement trailer if you'd like a look:

So, moar gits is comin' to get krumped. Sounds gud, ya?

Be sure to give em a very warm welcome once dey get 'ere 💥🔥

We'll try to get the next PC patch out ASAP. Until then, happy krumpin'

item.title: Hotfix 12.1 Patch Notes
item.description:
​GAMEPLAY AND BALANCING TWEAKS

Techmarine

  • Replenishing Salvo perk will not activate its cooldown when player has max equipment.

  • Reworked Battlefield Repairs perk. New description: “Any Squad Member restores 1 additional Armour Segment for each Finisher of Majoris or Extremis enemy.”

  • Inexorable Salvo perk buffed: ”The effect lasts for an additional 15 seconds after deactivation of ability.”

  • Decapitating Stratagem perk now correctly restores ability once it's fully empty. Previously it could restore ability after the first shot, if the player got a lucky kill, meaning that full ability restoration was mostly wasted.

  • Fixed cost of some Techmarine perks.

GENERAL FIXES

  • Crash fixes.

  • Fixed an issue that was blocking progress on Disruption (softlock after the Heldrake scene).

  • Added a voice preview in the voice selector menu.

  • Fixed an issue with Voice Pack VOs being not as loud as the rest of VOs.

  • Fixed an issue where Tarantula turrets could move a little bit after activation.

  • Lots of minor collision fixes on Disruption.

  • Lots of minor issues with various Battlefield Conditions.

  • Minor customisation fixes.

  • Minor localisation fixes.

  • Minor UI fixes.

item.title: Vermintide 2’s 8th Anniversary is live now! Double XP + A Quiet Drink!
item.description:
Heroes!

Another year passes us by but Vermintide is still going strong! Join our Heroes in yet another pub crawl through Helmgart in our 8th Anniversary! As always, for the duration of the event you will be able to access A Quiet Drink and there will be double XP!

The Anniversary Event is live now and will last until March 15!

New and old rewards!

As always, head over to the Weekly Event game mode in the Adventure mission selection menu to enter the event mission, ‘A Quiet Drink’. Completing the event with any difficulty will reward you with a brand-new anniversary portrait frame, ‘Feasts & Felicitations’!

There's nothing finer than spending time in good company.

Make sure to check the Event tab in Okri’s Challenges!

And for those of you who didn’t get the chance before, visit Lohner’s Emporium of Wonders’ ‘Event’ section for the limited time chance to purchase previous years’ Anniversary event rewards!

Without further ado…

Patch 6.10.0 patch notes
Features & Tweaks

  • Enabled the ‘A Quiet Drink’ event level, accessible through the Weekly Event game mode.

  • Added new anniversary event rewards.

  • Enabled Double XP.

  • Opened up the Event section in Lohner’s Emporium of Wonders for the duration of the Anniversary Event.

Fixes

  • Fixed an occasional crash when hitting things with a flail.

  • Fixed an issue where the blood tornadoes in Chaos Wastes were playing regular Blightstorm vortex sounds for clients, rather than the blood tornadoes’ unique sound effect.

  • Fixed a gift illusion for Sienna’s Scythe (from Gifts of the Wolf Father) being locked behind the Necromancer Deluxe Edition, rather than the regular Necromancer class DLC.
